_note: Original Practice questions aligned with CS Navigator's original Learn lesson for this topic.

questions.title: Async & Promises practice 1
questions.prompt: What does an async function always return?
choices: A Promise
A timer id
A callback
Only undefined
questions.explanation: JavaScript wraps a returned value in a fulfilled Promise, or returns a rejected Promise when the function throws.

questions.title: Async & Promises practice 2
questions.prompt: What happens when an awaited Promise rejects?
choices: An error is thrown at the await expression
The browser closes
Await returns null automatically
The function restarts
questions.explanation: Rejection enters the surrounding catch just like a synchronous throw, or rejects the async function when it is not caught.

questions.title: Trace Async & Promises
questions.prompt: What output does this code produce? Enter each line in order.
questions.explanation: The function waits for loadUser to settle, then prints either the returned name or a controlled failure message. The resulting output is 'Depends on the request result.'.

questions.title: Async & Promises good practice
questions.prompt: Which is a useful practice for Async & Promises?
choices: Await the operation that produces the needed value.
Skip input checks and assume every value is valid.
Hide every error and continue without checking the result.
Put all of the work in one long statement.
questions.explanation: Await the operation that produces the needed value. This keeps the code easier to understand and verify.

questions.title: Async & Promises practice 5
questions.prompt: Which version follows the lesson's safer approach?
choices:
const user = fetch("/api/user");
console.log(user.name);
Both versions are equally safe.
Neither version can run.
questions.explanation: Fetch returns a Promise, not the final user object. Await both the response and the later body-parsing Promise.

questions.title: Async & Promises practice 6
questions.prompt: Which statement best explains 'Fetch does not reject every bad response'?
choices: Skip checks and assume every value is valid.
A response with status 404 or 500 still fulfills the fetch Promise. Check `response.ok` and throw or return an error result before parsing data you expected from a successful request.
Hide errors and continue without checking the result.
Put every step into one long statement.
questions.explanation: A response with status 404 or 500 still fulfills the fetch Promise. Check `response.ok` and throw or return an error result before parsing data you expected from a successful request.

questions.title: Async & Promises practice 7
questions.prompt: Which is a useful practice for Async & Promises?
choices: Skip checks and assume every value is valid.
Hide errors and continue without checking the result.
Check HTTP response status explicitly.
Put every step into one long statement.
questions.explanation: Check HTTP response status explicitly. This makes the result easier to understand and verify.

questions.title: Async & Promises practice 8
questions.prompt: Which is a useful practice for Async & Promises?
choices: Skip checks and assume every value is valid.
Hide errors and continue without checking the result.
Put every step into one long statement.
Catch failures where recovery is possible.
questions.explanation: Catch failures where recovery is possible. This makes the result easier to understand and verify.

questions.title: Async & Promises practice 9
questions.prompt: Which is a useful practice for Async & Promises?
choices: Keep independent requests concurrent when appropriate.
Skip checks and assume every value is valid.
Hide errors and continue without checking the result.
Put every step into one long statement.
questions.explanation: Keep independent requests concurrent when appropriate. This makes the result easier to understand and verify.

questions.title: Async & Promises practice 10
questions.prompt: What output does this code produce? Enter each line in order.
questions.explanation: The function waits for loadUser to settle, then prints either the returned name or a controlled failure message. The output is 'Depends on the request result.'.
